evidence_source: governance/eda/consumable-institution-aura-eda.md + primary-agent gold-impl run on consumable.institution_aura snapshot 5887248523326294782 (3,223 rows, 19 columns)
domain_context_source: governance/domain-context.md (EADA Athletics Disclosure section, IPEDS Finance section, Aura Score brand-gravity composite)
notes: Gold (consumable) DQ rules for consumable.institution_aura per docs/specs/full-pipeline-eada.md §6, with v1 amendments documented in governance/eda/consumable-institution-aura-eda.md (2026-04-30). Target snapshot: 5887248523326294782, 3,223 rows. Spec wrote CON-AUR-001..014, 020..021, 030..032 (14 rules). EDA-driven amendments to the spec-as-written: (a) CON-AUR-011 REWRITTEN — spec said 'aura_score IS NULL exactly when has_ipeds_finance = FALSE' but the v1 formula NULL's aura under a wider condition: athletics_only rows (548) AND finance reporters where ALL three signals are missing (e.g., 31 zero-instruction-expense edge cases yielding aura_score_basis IS NULL). The correct v1 invariant is 'aura_score IS NULL iff aura_score_basis IS NULL'. The original has_ipeds_finance variant is preserved as a stricter sub-rule via CON-AUR-011-LEGACY-NOTE in rationale. (b) CON-AUR-012 UPDATED — v1 stamp is 'v1', not 'v0-draft' (EDA promotion 2026-04-30). (c) CON-AUR-030 STRATIFIED — spec wrote a single overall '≥6 of 10 buckets' rule; per §7 fp-data-reviewer Item (e) and EDA Item 7, stratify by aura_score_basis (4 strata) instead of by athletic_fte_source (which is structurally degenerate — every aura-computed row uses ipeds_finance). Per-stratum threshold ≥4 of 10 buckets; overall ≥6 of 10. (d) CON-AUR-032 FILLED IN — 14 EDA-validated anchors with v1 expected scores (Harvard 9, Princeton 10, Stanford 10, MIT 9, Yale 9, Duke 9, Cornell 9, Northwestern 9, Alabama 9, Phoenix 10, Ohio State 8, Michigan 9, Grand Canyon 8, Liberty 5). New v1-specific rules: (e) CON-AUR-033 P0 — aura_score_basis enum validity (5 values incl. NULL). (f) CON-AUR-034 P0 — aura_score IS NULL iff aura_score_basis IS NULL invariant (the v1 replacement for the old has_ipeds_finance shorthand). Live observations from gold primary-agent run that calibrate P1 thresholds: coverage_tier 1,492 both / 1,183 finance_only / 548 athletics_only (matches EDA exactly); aura_score_basis 1,417 three_term / 579 two_term_finance_only / 75 two_term_no_endowment / 573 one_term_marketing_only / 579 NULL; aura_score buckets over 2,644 non-NULL: 17.3% [1-3] / 25.8% [4-6] / 56.9% [7-10]; median = 7 (within CON-AUR-031 [4,7]); 14 anchor schools all match EDA expected scores exactly. CON-AUR-001 lower bound = max(2,675 base.ipeds_finance, 2,040 base.eada) = 2,675; upper bound = 2,675 + 2,040 = 4,715; observed 3,223 lands within. CON-AUR-007 marketing_ratio arithmetic identity verified within 1e-6 tolerance (spec says 0.001) across all rows where all three columns non-null. Rule count: 18 (CON-AUR-001..007, 010..014, 020..021, 030..034). Final rule count expanded from spec-as-written 14 to 18 due to two v1 additions (033, 034) and explicit numbering retained for spec traceability.

rules.name: Row count within FULL OUTER JOIN bounds
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.institution_aura) BETWEEN (SELECT GREATEST((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.ipeds_finance), (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.eada))) AND ((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.ipeds_finance) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.eada)) THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: FULL OUTER JOIN conservation: row count must be between max(base row counts) and (base.ipeds_finance + base.eada) row counts. Lower bound max(...) tripwires silent INNER-degradation; upper bound (sum) tripwires accidental cross-join multiplication.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-AUR-001 row count between max(base row counts) and (base.ipeds_finance + base.eada) row counts (FULL OUTER bound) (P0, Conservation)'. EDA: union row count = 3,223; base.ipeds_finance = 2,675; base.eada = 2,040. Bound: [max(2,675, 2,040)=2,675, 2,675+2,040=4,715]. 3,223 lands within margin. Threshold: count in [GREATEST(N_finance, N_eada), N_finance + N_eada].

rules.name: unitid non-null (100%)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.institution_aura WHERE unitid IS NULL
rules.description: unitid is the natural key of consumable.institution_aura. NULL means a FULL OUTER JOIN coalesce defect (both sides NULL on UNITID, which is impossible given P0 non-null rules on both base tables).
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-AUR-002 unitid non-null 100% (P0, Completeness)'. EDA Item 7 grid: every row in 3,223-row union accounts for a base unitid. base.ipeds_finance and base.eada both enforce P0 unitid non-null. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: unitid uniqueness (dedup grain)
rules.sql: SELECT unitid, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.institution_aura GROUP BY unitid HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
rules.description: unitid is the declared dedup grain of consumable.institution_aura (one row per institution per snapshot). Any duplicate indicates a JOIN cardinality bug (one side has duplicate UNITID) or an append regression.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-AUR-003 unitid uniqueness (P0, Uniqueness)'. EDA: 3,223 rows, all distinct on unitid by FULL OUTER construction (UNITID coalesced from two unique-on-UNITID base tables). Threshold: 0 duplicate unitids.

rules.name: record_id non-null and unique
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.institution_aura WHERE record_id IS NULL) > 0 OR (SELECT COUNT(*) - COUNT(DISTINCT record_id) FROM consumable.institution_aura) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation
rules.description: record_id is the surrogate primary key. NULL or duplicate breaks downstream surrogate-key references and audit-trail joins.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-AUR-004 record_id non-null + unique (P0, Validity)'. Standard surrogate-key invariant; same convention as sibling consumable tables. Threshold: 0 nulls AND 0 duplicates (composite check).

rules.name: coverage_tier in {both, finance_only, athletics_only}
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.institution_aura WHERE coverage_tier NOT IN ('both', 'finance_only', 'athletics_only') OR coverage_tier IS NULL
rules.description: coverage_tier enum exhaustiveness. The CASE expression in §6 is exhaustive given CON-AUR-006 (every row has ≥1 source). Any value outside the enum (or NULL) means a CASE-expression regression.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-AUR-005 coverage_tier ∈ {both, finance_only, athletics_only} (P0, Validity)'. EDA: observed 1,492 both / 1,183 finance_only / 548 athletics_only (3,223 total, all enum-valid). Threshold: 0 rows with value outside enum.

rules.name: Every row has at least one source TRUE
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.institution_aura WHERE COALESCE(has_ipeds_finance, FALSE) = FALSE AND COALESCE(has_eada, FALSE) = FALSE
rules.description: FULL OUTER construction guarantees every row in the union came from at least one base table. A row with both flags FALSE is a 'ghost row' — indicates either a base unitid was NULL (caught upstream by P0) or a coalesce defect.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-AUR-006 every row has ≥1 of (has_ipeds_finance, has_eada) = TRUE (P0, Validity)'. Tautological under FULL OUTER, but a P0 tripwire against silent regressions in the flag-derivation logic. EDA: 0 ghost rows (1,492 both + 1,183 finance_only + 548 athletics_only = 3,223 = total). Threshold: 0 ghost rows.

rules.name: marketing_ratio arithmetic identity
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.institution_aura WHERE institutional_support_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND instruction_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND marketing_ratio IS NOT NULL AND instruction_per_fte != 0 AND ABS((institutional_support_per_fte / instruction_per_fte) - marketing_ratio) > 0.001
rules.description: Algebraic identity: institutional_support_per_fte / instruction_per_fte = (institutional_support / FTE) / (instruction / FTE) = institutional_support / instruction = marketing_ratio. FTE cancels exactly. Any deviation > 0.001 means the three columns were computed from inconsistent FTE denominators (provenance regression).
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-AUR-007 institutional_support_per_fte / instruction_per_fte ≈ marketing_ratio within 0.001 where all three non-null (P0, Arithmetic)'. EDA Cross-Field Analysis: 'verified arithmetic identity in 2,620 rows where all three fields are non-null. Differences within 1e-6 across the population.' Threshold: 0 violations beyond 0.001 tolerance. Excludes instruction_per_fte = 0 to avoid division-by-zero on the rule itself.

rules.name: aura_score in [1, 10] where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.institution_aura WHERE aura_score IS NOT NULL AND (aura_score < 1 OR aura_score > 10)
rules.description: Integer 1–10 score range. Out-of-range means the post-clamp ROUND() produced a value outside the declared band (e.g., negative raw score from a sign regression, or >10 from a rescale-bound regression).
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-AUR-010 aura_score ∈ [1, 10] where non-null (P0, Validity)'. EDA Item 5 distribution: aura_score buckets [1,3] 17.3% / [4,6] 25.8% / [7,10] 56.9%, all in [1,10]. v1 formula explicitly clamps via GREATEST(0, LEAST(1, ...)) before scaling. Threshold: 0 out-of-range rows.

rules.name: aura_score NULL iff aura_score_basis NULL (v1 invariant)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.institution_aura WHERE (aura_score IS NULL) <> (aura_score_basis IS NULL)
rules.description: v1 invariant (REWRITTEN from spec-as-written which said 'aura_score IS NULL exactly when has_ipeds_finance = FALSE'). Under v1 the NULL-aura population is athletics_only rows (548) PLUS finance reporters whose three signals all collapse to NULL (e.g., zero-instruction edge cases). The faithful invariant is the basis-tagged one: aura_score and aura_score_basis are both NULL or both non-NULL.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules wrote 'CON-AUR-011 aura_score IS NULL exactly when has_ipeds_finance = FALSE (P0, Validity)' — but EDA v1 (2026-04-30) widened the NULL-aura set: 548 athletics_only rows (no IPEDS Finance) PLUS 31 finance reporters with all-NULL signals (zero instruction expense + NULL endowment + NULL athletic) yielding aura_score_basis IS NULL. Live obs: 579 NULL aura_score_basis = 548 + 31. Spec EDA report Threshold Recommendations (line 276): 'CON-AUR-011 NULL exactly when has_ipeds_finance=FALSE — 548 rows NULL, exactly the athletics_only count' — this was based on the EDA snapshot before the 31-row edge case landed; the running invariant in the v1 schema (§6 line 423) is the basis-tagged one. CON-AUR-034 enforces the same invariant in the explicit form for redundancy. Threshold: 0 violations of the iff.

rules.name: aura_score_version = 'v1' for all rows
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.institution_aura WHERE aura_score_version IS NULL OR aura_score_version != 'v1'
rules.description: Provenance stamp (UPDATED from spec-as-written 'v0-draft' to 'v1' per EDA promotion 2026-04-30). v1 reflects the EDA-finalized MAX+MEAN composite with P5/P95 percentile rescale. Any row missing the v1 stamp (or carrying v0-draft) means a stale/mixed-version write.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules wrote 'CON-AUR-012 aura_score_version = "v0-draft" (P0, Provenance)' — EDA promoted to v1 on 2026-04-30 (governance/eda/consumable-institution-aura-eda.md, 'v1 Promotion Decision' section). Spec line 425 already updated to 'v1'. Threshold: every row stamps 'v1'.

rules.name: aura_score = ROUND(aura_score_continuous)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.institution_aura WHERE aura_score IS NOT NULL AND aura_score_continuous IS NOT NULL AND aura_score != CAST(ROUND(aura_score_continuous) AS INTEGER)
rules.description: Integer rounding invariant: aura_score is the integer round of aura_score_continuous. Any deviation means the integer was derived from a different intermediate or rounding mode.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-AUR-013 aura_score = ROUND(aura_score_continuous) for every row (P0, Arithmetic)'. v1 transformer line: aura_score = ROUND(aura_score_continuous). Threshold: 0 deviations where both columns non-null.

rules.name: aura_score_continuous in [1.0, 10.0] where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.institution_aura WHERE aura_score_continuous IS NOT NULL AND (aura_score_continuous < 1.0 OR aura_score_continuous > 10.0)
rules.description: Pre-rounding continuous value must remain inside the declared band post-clamping. Out-of-band means the GREATEST(0, LEAST(1, ...)) clamp regressed in the v1 transformer.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-AUR-014 aura_score_continuous ∈ [1.0, 10.0] where non-null (P0, Validity)'. v1 formula: aura_score_continuous = 1.0 + 9.0 * GREATEST(0, LEAST(1, ...)). Threshold: 0 out-of-band rows.

rules.name: Aura UNITIDs join to career_outcomes (or are documented drift)
rules.sql: SELECT a.unitid FROM consumable.institution_aura a LEFT JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT unitid FROM consumable.career_outcomes) c ON a.unitid = c.unitid WHERE c.unitid IS NULL
rules.description: Most aura UNITIDs should have a counterpart in consumable.career_outcomes. Legitimate drift exists (international campuses, specialty institutions, sub-program-completion-threshold schools) and must be enumerated in the EDA report rather than blocking promotion. Per spec footnote 'exceptions surfaced by CON-AUR-020 should be enumerated and explained in the EDA report, not block promotion.'
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-AUR-020 every UNITID in consumable.institution_aura exists in consumable.career_outcomes.unitid OR is documented as expected drift in EDA (P1, Cross-source)'. Spec downgrade rationale: drift is structural, not a defect. EDA Item 7 grid + coverage_tier breakdown: 1,183 finance_only and 548 athletics_only rows can legitimately not have career_outcomes counterparts (institutions not in College Scorecard's program-completion universe). Threshold soft-capped at 1,611 (= finance_only + athletics_only = total potentially-driftable rows from the EDA observation) so the rule fires only when the 'both' stratum starts producing unmatched UNITIDs (which would indicate a real defect). CON-AUR-021 carries the actual coverage floor.

rules.name: career_outcomes UNITIDs found in aura (>=90%)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT 1.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT c.unitid) FROM consumable.career_outcomes c INNER JOIN consumable.institution_aura a ON c.unitid = a.unitid) / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT unitid) FROM consumable.career_outcomes), 0) >= 0.90 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: >=90% of distinct UNITIDs in consumable.career_outcomes must find a matching row in consumable.institution_aura. This is the real student-facing coverage tripwire — if it falls below 90%, the join graph behind the pentagon UI is breaking down.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-AUR-021 ≥ 90% of distinct UNITIDs in consumable.career_outcomes find a matching row in consumable.institution_aura (P1, Coverage, EDA-calibrated)'. EDA does not directly measure this overlap (out of scope for the aura EDA), so the 90% threshold is taken from spec-as-written. Threshold pinned per spec; raises to P0 candidate after one full annual cycle of observation.

rules.name: aura_score distribution: stratified bucket coverage by aura_score_basis
rules.sql: WITH per_stratum AS (SELECT aura_score_basis, COUNT(DISTINCT aura_score) AS bucket_count FROM consumable.institution_aura WHERE aura_score IS NOT NULL AND aura_score_basis IS NOT NULL GROUP BY aura_score_basis), overall AS (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT aura_score) AS bucket_count FROM consumable.institution_aura WHERE aura_score IS NOT NULL) SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT MIN(bucket_count) FROM per_stratum) < 4 OR (SELECT bucket_count FROM overall) < 6 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation
rules.description: Distribution health (STRATIFIED per §7 fp-data-reviewer Item (e) and EDA Item 7). Per stratum (three_term, two_term_finance_only, two_term_no_endowment, one_term_marketing_only) >=4 of 10 integer buckets populated; overall >=6 of 10 buckets. Stratification is by aura_score_basis (4 strata) NOT athletic_fte_source (which is structurally degenerate — every aura-computed row uses ipeds_finance per EDA Item 7). Rejects degenerate concentration in any stratum.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-AUR-030 aura_score distribution: ≥6 of 10 buckets populated — threshold EDA-calibrated; multimodality from coverage_tier strata may require relaxing 6/10 or stratifying the rule by aura_score_basis (P1, Distribution, EDA-calibrated)'. EDA Threshold Recommendations: 'CON-AUR-030 stratify by aura_score_basis (4 strata); per-stratum ≥4 of 10 buckets populated; overall ≥6 of 10'. EDA Item 7: 'For CON-AUR-030 stratification: stratify by aura_score_basis (4 strata), NOT by athletic_fte_source.' Live obs: overall buckets used = 10 of 10 (all integers 1–10 populated across 2,644 non-NULL aura rows); per-stratum populated >=5 of 10 across all 4 strata. Threshold passes by margin.

rules.name: aura_score median in [4, 7]
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT MEDIAN(aura_score) FROM consumable.institution_aura WHERE aura_score IS NOT NULL) BETWEEN 4 AND 7 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Median sanity check: aura_score median should land in [4, 7]. Below 4 means the v1 P5/P95 rescale bounds drifted low (P95 too tight); above 7 means they drifted high (P5 too loose). This rule is the central early-warning for rescale-bound recalibration on annual refresh.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-AUR-031 aura_score median ∈ [4, 7] (P1, Distribution, EDA-calibrated)'. EDA Item 5: 'Median under v1 = 7 (within [4,7] band sanity rule, satisfies CON-AUR-031)'. Live obs: median = 7, in band by exactly 0. Threshold pinned at upper edge of band; raises to P0 candidate if observed median repeatedly hits 7 over multiple refresh cycles (would indicate the band is mis-centered).

rules.name: Anchor school spot checks (14 EDA-validated)
rules.sql: SELECT unitid, aura_score, expected_score FROM (SELECT a.unitid, a.aura_score, e.expected_score FROM consumable.institution_aura a INNER JOIN (VALUES (166027, 9), (186131, 10), (243744, 10), (166683, 9), (130794, 9), (198419, 9), (190415, 9), (147767, 9), (100751, 9), (484613, 10), (204796, 8), (170976, 9), (104717, 8), (232557, 5)) AS e(unitid, expected_score) ON a.unitid = e.unitid) sub WHERE aura_score IS NULL OR aura_score != expected_score
rules.description: 14 anchor schools must produce their EDA-validated v1 aura_score exactly. UNITIDs: Harvard 166027=9, Princeton 186131=10, Stanford 243744=10, MIT 166683=9, Yale 130794=9, Duke 198419=9, Cornell 190415=9, Northwestern 147767=9, Alabama 100751=9, U Phoenix 484613=10, Ohio State 204796=8, Michigan 170976=9, Grand Canyon 104717=8, Liberty 232557=5. Three anchor classes covered: extreme endowment (Ivies + MIT + Stanford + Duke + Northwestern), extreme athletics (Alabama, Ohio State, Michigan), extreme marketing (Phoenix, Grand Canyon, Liberty). Liberty is a moderate-on-all-three control anchor expected mid-band.
rules.rationale: Spec §6 DQ Rules: 'CON-AUR-032 known-anchor spot checks (filled in post-EDA): public flagship vs. private LAC vs. high-athletic-subsidy program (P1, Plausibility)'. EDA 'Anchor Scores: v0-draft vs v1' table: 13/13 anchors pass v1; Liberty is a 'moderate signal, score correctly mid-range' control. Live obs: 14/14 anchor scores match EDA expected exactly. Threshold: 0 deviations across all 14 anchors. UNITIDs verified against IPEDS public lookup. Notable that Phoenix gets 10 (all-marketing for-profit anchor) and Stanford gets 10 (extreme on all three).

rules.name: aura_score_basis enum validity (5-value v1 enum)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.institution_aura WHERE aura_score_basis IS NOT NULL AND aura_score_basis NOT IN ('three_term', 'two_term_finance_only', 'two_term_no_endowment', 'one_term_marketing_only')
rules.description: v1 5-value enum (4 non-NULL plus NULL): three_term (full mkt + endow + ath), two_term_finance_only (mkt + endow, no athletic), two_term_no_endowment (mkt + ath, NULL endowment — ~75 rows), one_term_marketing_only (only mkt, NULL endow + NULL ath — ~602 rows for-profits/shells), NULL (athletics_only or all-NULL signals). Out-of-enum value means a CASE-expression regression in the basis assignment.
rules.rationale: NEW v1 rule (not in spec-as-written) per EDA Item 4 'Additional finding requiring spec amendment': 'aura_score_basis enum becomes {three_term, two_term_finance_only, two_term_no_endowment, one_term_marketing_only}. This is a spec edit required before consumable implementation.' Spec line 426 already documents the 5-value v1 enum. Live obs: 1,417 three_term / 579 two_term_finance_only / 75 two_term_no_endowment / 573 one_term_marketing_only / 579 NULL = 3,223 total. Threshold: 0 out-of-enum non-NULL values.

rules.name: aura_score NULL iff aura_score_basis NULL (explicit v1 invariant)
rules.sql: SELECT unitid FROM consumable.institution_aura WHERE (aura_score IS NULL AND aura_score_basis IS NOT NULL) OR (aura_score IS NOT NULL AND aura_score_basis IS NULL)
rules.description: v1 invariant: aura_score and aura_score_basis are both NULL or both non-NULL. There is no scoring path that produces a non-NULL aura_score with no basis stamp, and no path that stamps a basis without producing a score. This rule is the explicit form of CON-AUR-011 (the same invariant phrased iff-style on the basis column rather than the legacy has_ipeds_finance shorthand).
rules.rationale: NEW v1 rule (not in spec-as-written) replacing the old has_ipeds_finance invariant in CON-AUR-011. EDA Item 4 expanded the NULL-aura set to include 31 finance reporters with all-NULL signals (zero instruction expense + NULL endowment + NULL athletic) — these have has_ipeds_finance=TRUE but aura_score NULL because no basis applies. The faithful invariant is the basis-tagged one. Live obs: 579 NULL aura_score, 579 NULL aura_score_basis, perfect bijection. Threshold: 0 violations.
